message:
fix(net/dns): extract hostname validation, add per-label 63-byte length check, validate before cache

The DNS resolver had two validation gaps:

1. Hostname validation was duplicated between lookup_host() and
   cached_lookup_host() with slightly different logic, and
   lookup_host() skipped validation entirely (relying on the
   downstream nameserver to reject bad names).

2. Individual DNS labels were not validated against the RFC 1035
   63-byte limit — only the overall 253-byte hostname limit was
   checked.

Fix: New validate_lookup_hostname() extracts the shared validation
logic with both the 253-byte total limit and a per-label 63-byte
limit check.  Both lookup_host() and cached_lookup_host() call it
before any cache interaction, ensuring invalid hostnames are
rejected before touching the cache or network.

Tests:
- resolver_invalid_hostname_fails_before_cache_lookup: validates
  that a poisoned cache entry for an invalid hostname is never
  consulted — the validation rejects the lookup before the cache
  is checked.
- Tests for overlength labels (>63 bytes), empty labels, and
  various edge cases.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
